column2:
A hush in dusk, a velvet silhouette that stalks the seam of night,
silvered paws afloat the air, whispering secrets to the moon.
Whiskered compass, velvet heartbeat, eyes like distant lanterns guiding dreams,
a dancer who folds into sunbeams, then vanishes where rugs remember.

A silver needle threads the morning between cloud and cliff,
stitches distant hiss and heartbeat of the blue into silver glare.
It wears a memory of winds like unspoken names,
and in its hollow chest, a quiet roar rehearses gravity’s surrender.
Glide, then vanish—an iron swallow autographing the sky,
leaving footprints of light where silence learns to travel.

Sketcher of dawn in a tremble of air, noonday’s whisperer with a throat of bells.
Feathered sundial that marks the wind’s shortcuts, stitching time to fleeting shadows.
Ululations of sun on wing-tip, a chorus stitched to the shrinking blue.
Clutch of leaves and a pulse of sparrow-syllables, telling the sky its own name.

Mist, in velvet dusk, I glimpse a quiet impulse of grace,
a pale flame moving softly where the fern sighs and the pale grasses bend.
Antlers whisper through the hush like branches rehearsing winter songs,
hooves sketching careful music on the frost-thin air.
Eyes hold the forest’s secret map, a sudden breath of dawn on velvet tongue,
and when the world exhales, it dissolves into whispers of fur and breath.

Above the listening clouds it gathers speed, a breath of steel and sky-woven light.
A silver sigh cuts dawn in two, measuring distances with a measured hum.
Its bones remember routes of wind, stitching maps across thin pinions of sun.
Windows glimmer like quiet moons, inviting souls to trade gravity for whispered horizons.
In its wake, the towns exhale and tilt, as if the map had learned a new heartbeat.
It laughs in the vocabulary of engines, a distant thunder speaking in crisp precision.
Landing is a gentle bow to gravity, a hush where footprints borrow the afternoon.

I wear a chorus in a delicate throat, a rumor of dawn on quick feet,
I trade the air’s ledger for a pocketful of sunlit notes, so bright they bow the wind.
Feathered thirst for ledges and distant bells, I sip the morning from a whispering cup,
A road-map of shadows stitched to the sky, I stitch the daylight into a small, bright breath.

A silver sigh cuts through the blue, a tongue of wind
That wears a clock in its belly and bells for lungs.
It trades the ground for a chrome memory of clouds,
Tracing maps of heat and silence in the air’s throat.
Steel armor cradles a feverish heartbeat, high as a starless city,
Carrying strangers' prayers in a pouch of rushing dawn. 
Wings skim the hush where horizons forget to blink.

A sable silhouette drifts along the moonlit sill, velvet steps stealing sound.
Whisker-tine alarms the dark with a tremor, a silvered radar on fur.
Eyes like lanterns sleep-laden, catching secrets from shadowed corners of the room.
A chorus of purrs—low engines of comfort—stirs the air with warm, unseen hands.
It folds into silence as the evening sighs, a soft archivist of quiet hours.
Feathers of dusk cling to its breath, while claws trace stars upon unseen maps.

In silver dawns, a whisper wears amber sleeves,
gliding through needles of green, a ghost of breath and tremor.
Antlers loom like moonlit branches, cautious sentinels of hush,
hooves sketching rain on moss, a heartbeat threaded with the pines.
Eyes hold the forest’s quiet weather, a tremor and a resolve,
the light bends where it pauses, then vanishes into the naming mist.

A glint of dawn threads through a chorus of rumor and air,
feathers as weathered sails catching a sunlit breath.
A listener of branches, a clock in a throat of wind,
taxing the blue with quiet arithmetic of flit and fold.
Windowpanes remember when a whisper stitched the air to a hymn,
and the sky wore a secret bright as a pocketful of rain.

A steel heartbeat hums above the whispering earth, slicing clouded silk with silver breath.
A waking arrow remembers wind, pressing morning into a distant blue memory.
Windows gleam like eyes of a sleeping god, tallying miles where maps forget their names.
It wears the day on its sleeve—lanterns for eyes, a hollowed roar that sings of routes unseen.

Lungfuls of sun and rain chase the same brisk breath along a restless yard,
a tail drawing maps in dust, a compass of warmth circling ankles,
tongue tasting every breeze like a failed poem tasting morning,
loyal shadow rehearsing joy in quick, bright eruptions of the heart.

Moonlit hunter, velvet shadow tipping the dusk,  
whispers of fur gliding through a room like tide-washed air.  
Sparrow-thief of shadows, with bells that barely ring,  
a quiet throne of sunlit heat, curling at the seam of cushions.  
Eyes lanterns in a dusk-wedroom, sudden hush, soft paws rehearsing the night.  
Latch-key whiskers map the air, a hush-tattoo on the floor.

Dlicking daylight through a kiss of chrome, it wears a second skin of glass.
A heartbeat hums in quiet rhythm, lungs of steel drawing road-sky inward.
Sparks braid with rubber ankles, whispering prayers to the wind’s turning wheels.
It learns the world by listening to its own quiet thunder, and glides.

Metal belly hums a stubborn hymn, backlit by a ribbon of headlights.
Boulders of kindness hold your payloads, like secrets tucked in a heavy apron.
Tires murmur stories to the road, a tireless heartbeat through the dull, rust-colored dawn.
The mirror-bright face watches far-off grocery-lit towns, carrying weather and wishes in its wake.

A shadow slips through the hush of pines, tan as leaves in autumn’s breath,
hooves tapping a lanterned rhythm on the mossy floor, shy as dusk.
Antlers time-worn branches above a tremor of velvet thunder,
eyes like quiet tarnished glass, sensing the wind’s pale rumor.
A breathing silhouette that tastes the air with careful mercy,
scarlet moments vanish where the brush remembers, and memories bloom.

A wind-thrown shadow on the wind-touched grass, she bends the meadow to her will.
Hooves drum syllables of thunder hushed by twilight, a fever of sudden speed in noble shoulders.
The sun wears her as a living weather, braided sunbeams clinging to a prancing spine.
Nostrils flare like smoky bells, lungs singing in syllables of ground, release, and flight.
Her gaze keeps the horizon honest, a patient rider in the pulse between heartbeat and rail.
She is scent and sinew, a lineage of winter storms and open plains, returning us to wild maps.

A flash of ember in a waking attic breeze,
a pocketful of dawn stitched tight with careful wing.
It threads the noon with silver lightning, quick as memory,
and wears a chorus like rain clinging to a windowsill.
Feathers hush the sky’s tremor, a small compass of song,
while gold-tipped claws sketch poems on the air.

A bronze heart beating in the pond’s glassy hush,
green lanterns blink from mud-framed lanterns of sky.
It speaks in leaps, a chorus of ripples and tucked breaths,
sudden bells from underwater lungs, then silence—breathless, bright.

A waking needle threads the morning blue, then sifts through thundered quiet.
Metal wings hum a distant lullaby, craving the shimmer of dawn on skin.
Steel lungs gulp gusts, counting miles like beads slipping from a bracelet.
In a silver cradle, eyes map horizons, while the world shrinks to pinpoints below.

A whisper of shadow limns the room with velvet feet,
eyes like moonlit coals that sip the quiet air.
Silken hush travels on a warm, restless tide,
while a secret hunter curls beneath the chair.
Whiskers sketch the lantern-night, a tremor in the dark,
and velvet paws compose soft thunder on the floor.

A rumor of thunder with skin of lacquered dawn,
It gathers breath in a chest of humming rain,
Two glass eyes blink promises to the street,
A slow heartbeat carved in wheels that hum along the seam of night.
Metal sinews flex and stretch the air into shapes of distance,
Scent of new rain and gasoline lingers in its wake.

A living compass of eager rain and road,
whose pulse drums crusted partners to the wind.
A warm lantern circling ankles, rain-slicked fur,
tongue tasting every shadow in the park’s green chorus.
Eyes map every sigh, return with bright, foolish cheer,
a heartbeat that runs ahead, then lingers at the door.

A sun-worn giant of appetite and miles,
Muscles of steel hum under weathered skin,
It drums the road with a heartbeat of diesel sighs,
Carrying secrets in a ribbed, open chest of cargo. 
Its shoulders bear the weight of distant horizons,
A quiet, constant laborer in the throat of dawn.

Fleeting grace stitched by dapples of dawn, it treads the frost-thin edge of the pine.
 Eyes, lanterns saved from winter, measure the hush between breath and breathless light.
 Whispered antlers ripple through thorn-bright thickets, a secret language of quiet storms.
 Hoof-steps fold the meadow into wind, and rumor follows in the stillness of green.

A narrow flame stitched to the morning breeze,
it stitches sky with whisper-thin, bright thread.
Feathers carry a clockwork heartbeat, quick,
tinny bells in the throat sing of rain and sun.
It kneels on wind-denied branches, then lifts—
a silver sigh dissolving into open air.

Bright nose tracing the morning air, a compass of scent and sun.
A chorus of footsteps; eager, returning to the rhythm of a whistle.
Warm harbor of fur, weathered by laughter and rain, quick to greet a fault with forgiveness.
Eyes that know the shape of footsteps before the door remembers to open.

A dawn-furred thunder along the meadow’s breath,  
Muscles humming hidden bells beneath sunlit skin,  
A briar-thirst for wind stitched into every stride,  
Hooves beating maps into earth, releasing dawn.  
A patient silhouette where thunder learns to listen,  
Harnessed with light and wild, unspoken will,  
Nostrils flare like distant drums in the rain.

Metal throat hums a blistering lullaby,  
steel wings sketching pale mirrors against the noon.  
A clockwork heartbeat climbs the ladder of clouds,  
windows for eyes drink sun with a thirsting tongue.  
Whispers of thunder trail the wake left in blue velvet,  
lanterns blink in tandem with distant, breathless stars.  
Between ground and sky, a restless pilgrim finds its routes,  
and gravity loosens its grip for a brief, silver-spun dream.

A quiet beast that wakes when dawn takes breath,
its hollow chest drums softly to the sea.
Cloth sails stretched like sighs against the wind,
a clockwork pilgrim tallying miles with foam.
It wears a compass smile in starlit scales,
and mouths of rigging whisper to distant bays.
Yearning for the painted edge where land forgets its name,
it learns the language of tides and turning horizons.

Beneath a lid of rain-slick emerald skin, it leaks the evening’s music with a sighing throat,  
lantern eyes keeping quiet watch where shadowed waters dream of insects’ bright bodies.  
A lantern-jump, a tremor of green heartbeat, a chorus stitched in damp air and lilting ribs,  
sun-warmed pulse slips away in a ripple, pockets of moss holding its quiet, patient secrets.

A chorus of rumble and lacquered dusk, it breathes in steel-blue lungs,
Bearing the weight of miles like a patient drumbeat on the road.
Its shadow stamps the gravel with a stubborn heartbeat,
A chest of boxes whispering parcels to the listening sky.
Wind gnaws at its teeth, chrome glints like weathered teeth after a long vow,
And in its groan, distant towns lean closer, hungry for the weary song it carries.

A metal heartbeat humming along a sunlit spine,
teeth of color bite the air, a velvet growl of motion.
It wears its lanterns like impatient stars, blinking through glass breath,
and skims the city’s skin, a quiet thunder in disguise.
Its lungs inhale the road, exhale a silver whisper of speed,
while doors slide open like doors to evening’s private rooms.

A silhouette of dusk with velvet footprints, soft as rain on slate.
Whispers of moon-kissed breath curl through a sunless room, a patient heat.
Eyes like lanterns that blink in the hush between sighs and shadowed creases.
A hunter’s grace in silence, velvet-tailed, threading the air with quick, bright dreams.
Whiskers map the room’s conspiracies, tasting the air for tremors of a sigh.
Paws ghost through sunbeams, leaving warmth as tiny, unspoken promises.
In the cradle of night, a quiet sovereign rouses, then folds into velvet again.

In moonlit hush, a quiet archer threads the north wind’s breath,
tender brows brushed by morning reeds, a hush of velvet steps.
Eyes like frost-melt pearls survey the shadowed fringe of pines,
antlers crown the dusk with whispers of old winters’ ash and mist.
A heartbeat of rustle, hooves sketching the silvered earth in prayer,
the forest wears a secret mouth for listening stars.

I wake the wind with a silver heartbeat, loud but hushed in breathless blue,
steel bones singing of distant harbors, charting whispers from the sun.
Lanterns for eyes blink in a throat of glass, where maps spill like rain on skin,
a hunter of clouds glides through a quiet, metal sky without a footprint on the dawn.

A morning painter waits on slick green skin, dew-brushed and speckled with mossy sun.
He rehearses leaps in a chorus of muddy questions, lungs answering with a chorus-prime.
Pale moonlight slipper-slips on his ribbed armor, a pond becomes a silver stage.
Tongue a sudden ribbon, tasting life at the edge of tremulous reeds and rain.

A steel heartbeat hums along a waking road,  
wagon-worn lungs breathing exhaust sighs in the dawn.  
A brick-red courage carried on gravel-tempered feet,  
muscle-formed ribs cradle cargo with patient gravity.  

Windows wink like eyes under a weathered brow,  
tail-lights blink Morse in the quiet of follow-me lanes.  
It listens to the rumble of miles, gathering stories  
and returns each night stained with the map of journeys.

A creature of dawn, thunder in a quiet frame,
muscle and breath stitching the wind to a single name.
Hooves drum the earth like rain on a copper bowl,
manes braid the sunset, whisking shadows from the whole.
A loyal flame in the throat of open fields,
it moves as tide and stubborn will concedes.

A glint of dusk in a pocket of wind, I listen for commas in the sky.
Feathers sketch whispers against a blue memoranda, a clockwork lullaby.
Tiny punctuations alight on wire and leaf, signing syllables in bright breath.
A lacquered note stitches morning to night, then folds into a quiet, waking beat.

A warm shadow with a keen, damp nose tracing the scent of rain on ground.
Gentle thunder in a chest of fur, bounding breath sparking sparks in pale dawn air.
A tail like weathered pennant, wagging maps of every familiar street.
 fidelity in a grin, eyes bright as morning frost, returning every paused heartbeat.

A steel heartbeat hums along the stubborn seam of night,
glossed window eyes drink pavement’s quick, thirsty light.
cold breath sniffs wind at every corner’s edge,
muscle-born thunder in a chest of lacquered red.
it wears a mile-long grin of chrome and shadow,
and drums a secret rhythm on the tongue of tires.
it speaks in lantern sighs, a traveling mirror,
carrying maps of every street it ever learned.

Drinking the wind like a long, patient memory,
her ribs hum with the clockwork of tides,
canvas sighs in the quartered dusk, a curtain of rain-silver,
lanterns blink in the wake, a rumor of distant bells.

Her heart keeps rhythm with the sway of two oceans,
mirror-still eyes chart horizons that never rest,
and the seam between sky and seam between waves
holds a quiet oath: to carry the unspoken toward dawn.

I tread the edge of thickets where breath thins to wind,
hooves whisper to frost as dusk taps its silver drum.
Antlers, cathedral branches poised against the pale wood sky,
a pulse of velvet dusk in motion, soft as rain on fur.
Eyes like quiet lanterns, catching moonlit secrets of the glade,
I move, a shy silhouette stitched to the earth’s own sigh.

A dawn-soft silhouette threads through the sunlit dust,
whispers of velvet steps that hush the clock’s harsh shout.
Eyes like fallen moons ignite the room with patient flame,
and fur, the quiet weathering of rain against a dream.
A hunter’s patience curled inside a velvet cup of night,
and in the nap’s curled syllables, a warm, secret pulse takes flight.

A green hush among the reeds, a midnight splash of sighs,
webbed shadows quilt the moonlit pond with whispered leap.
A chorus stitched from lilting ripples and dampened dawn,
the lilac throat bellows songs that swell like rain on lilypads.
Tiny lungs bloom in chorus of bug-haunted air,
eyes like jeweled lanterns blinking secrets to the surface.

A flare of color stitched to a morning breath,
it threads the air with quiet, wind-woven trails.
A supple metronome against the tremor of branches,
tiny drumbeats drum the dawn into listening ears.
Glass-gliding sighs lift it above the busy map of roofs,
and in its echo, the sky keeps a small, bright hinge.

A swollen chest of steel, a mouth that breathes in iron scent,
It drags its weathered sigh across the seam of dawn.
Rumbles like distant thunder fixed in a stubborn throat,
A citadel that stores the day’s weather, loads of gravel-dark and light.
Its eyes glow amber, watching the horizon gulp its own miles,
And in the quiet between end and beginning, it hums a road-song.

A wind-touched shadow gathering thunder in its breath,
hooves striking drums on earth’s echo, quick as dawn.
Muscle and grace braid fire along a silent path,
maned cape of dusk that tastes the scent of rain.
A partner to the road, a whisper between rein and will,
gliding through fields where the sun keeps balance on its arc.

A quiet bell wakes at dawn, a wooden breath sighing salt.
White sails inhale the early wind, counting stars like distant coins.
Her heartbeat drums against the harbor's apron, a patient, roaming tempo.
She keeps her secrets in that bruise-blue belly where the compass dreams.

A chariot of thirsting hums beneath the skin of dusk,  
It wears a mirror for a heartbeat and a mouth that drinks the road.  
Its lungs breathe tin-sweet thunder, drawing breath from streetlight lamps,  
And in its wake, the chorus of tires hums like rain on glass.

Moonlight threads through a velvet silhouette, patient as dusk.
A whisper of fur caresses the quiet rug, soft as sighs.
Eyes like amber lanterns flicker in the hush between breaths.
Paws draw careful rivers on carpet shores, then vanish in a sigh.

A silver needle threads the hush between ground and cloud,
whispering maps to strangers as it climbs the tongue of light.
Its breath draws lines of weather on a quiet throat,
carrying city dreams in a humming, human breath.
Metal wings become echoes of thunder teased into calm,
a distant clock counting miles that forgets to land.

Hushed feet stitched through the frost-lit meadow, pale-kissed and alert,  
eyes like twin frost moons glancing where the pines lean close in prayer.  
Antlers, winter-touched constellations, glimmer with storm-light in the dusk,  
a whisper of breath brushes the fern, then vanishes into the breath of trees.

A jumper of dusk-green secrets, plump with moonlit sighs,
it wears a chorus on its throat and hops in lyric tides.
Mud perfumes its breath, rain drums its pulse on reeds,
eyes like lanterns waking wells where quiet rivers plead.
It keeps counsel with the night, a glimmering oath in slime—
a small drummer of marsh whispers, living between the rhyme.

A flicker of dawn stitched into a slender throat,  
waking the needle-thin wind with a jeweled trill.  
Cloth of sun on a stealthy sash, it threads the air,  
nests of breath swelling where the shadow tips its hat.

A heartbeat on a leash, grass underfoot sings in quick, bright inches.
Fluids of sun and earth cling to whiskered weather, nose tracing dawn's cold trail.
A devoted shadow answers every clap of air with a fevered, wagging rumor.
Heat in the chest, a compass of scent, loyally spinning toward the friendly wind.
Miles become breath, and every hello is a feast of listening ears.
Eyes glint like weathered coins, lending warmth to chilly thresholds.
The dusk witnesses a soft, fearless chorus—happiness stamped in a common, unspoken name.

A clang of iron lungs rolling toward the dawn,  
hinges sighing like secrets kept in heavy rain;  
furrows of dust trace its patient pilgrimage,  
bright-eyed in the glare, a heartbeat on wheels.  

It carries miles in settled shoulders, quiet thunder  
rumbling lullabies to towns sleeping in the map’s pocket;  
dust-souled companion, bearing the weight of unspoken hours,  
a slow, stubborn ache of service under the open sky.

A dusk-hued impulse gathers thunder under skin,
hooves drum quiet maps into the field’s pale breath.
Manes whisper weather, the wind's own confidant,
bridled winter in the shoulder’s tempered poise.
Eyes hold dawn, a patient compass drawing routes,
silk-sinewed rhythm threading through open time.

Moonlit whiskers tremble at the edge of dawn,
a whispering shadow learns the rug’s small map.
Eyes like lost lanterns blink in velvet dusk,
soft paws sketch silence, a patient, patient clock.
Whisked by moonbeams, small hunts of breath and grace,
the heartbeat drums a secret, velvet rain.
Slinking purrs bloom where velvet shadows kiss,
and dusk keeps watch until the sun remembers its name.

A quiet giant with timber lungs hums to the tide,
carrying a memory of oaths stitched in knot and seam.
Horizon breathes along its ribs, a gull-thread at the bow,
its teeth ring with the chorus of ropes and rusted bells.
It learns the weather in its bones, and rain crowns its brow.
Lanterns blink like distant stars, guiding a patient heartbeat home.

A silver sigh splits the morning’s breath, cutting a pale thread through clouds.
It wears a hollow roar like distant thunder learned in a ballroom of steel.
Glass lungs inhale the map of rivers, exhale a heartbeat of distant towns.
Feathers of brightness trail its wake, a rumor of wings that learned gravity’s code.
It hunts the sunrise with quiet wheels and letters etched in the blue above.
A memory of altitude lingers where the sky keeps its secret address.

A quiet silhouette where shadowed pines convene,
limbs fluent as wind over frost-thick leaves.
A pale crown glints like frost in morning breath,
and eyes hold the hush of far-running streams.
Hooves beat a secret hymn on frozen moss,
antlers braid the amber dusk with woodland light.
The meadow sighs as dusk slips into her coat,
and the forest keeps her gaze in velvet silence.

A pocket of rain in a tiny green herald’s skin,
he wears the night’s damp chorus as a crown of breath,
a chorus of damp stones marking his lilting prose.
Soft lanterns blink on his tongue, a moonlit leap between reeds,
he keeps the pond’s pulse in a pocket of hush and bounce,
mirth stitched to ankles, skin listening for the meeting of rain and earth.

At dawn, a sinew of sound stitched to the waking air,
Feathers quilted with morning light hum its quiet anthem.
It wears the sun on its back, a spark of embered breath,
A court of small green echoes circles the open window. 
Cliff-thin notes braid the breeze, a map without ink,
And the tiny compass of hunger or hope tilts toward the sky.

A weathered compass lopes along the fence of noon,
sniffing the world for whispered secrets in the grass.
Tongue-laughs hang like pennants from a sun-warmed jaw,
eyes bright as lanterns when the trail turns uphill.

A heartbeat that drums the air with muddy yips and paw-slowed dances,
loyal tides returning to a single, patient shore.
Soft storms of fur become a compass rose of trust,
and the yard sighs, knowing a faithful track will lead it home.

A born wanderer of roads, he hums in even breaths, metal lungs and tire-song.
Rear lanterns glow like patient sunsets, cargo dreams tucked beneath a broad back.
He paws at concrete, steady as a heartbeat, swallowing miles with a gloved, grinning mouth.
The dawn finds him listening to tremors of weight, a quiet drumline across the earth.

I wake to a chorus stitched in dawn light, a small flame in the blue,
Feathers catch rumors of wind, delicate as whispered lace.
I hear a heartbeat of maples, a quicksilver rustle in the eaves,
A compass of beaks and lullabies that stitch the air with silver thread.
It tucks away the sun in a woven nest of breath and timing,
Then threads the horizon with a bright, reverent sigh and flight.

A chariot of breath on four sly lungs, it hums in polished dawns,
lean muscles curling through streets, a metal heartbeat veining tar.
Its whisper wears tires like quiet hooves on glassy rivers,
glimmering eyes of glass blink codes to the unseen tracks.
It tastes of distant rain and oil, a traveler’s lunch of borrowed hours,
and when it stops, the world exhales, doors sighing like relieved wings.

A bright-tailed shadow that bounds on warm afternoons,
language of twitched ears, velvet tongue, and wet-nosed greetings.
It wears the sun like a collar and chases the wind for news,
steadfast heart drumbeat hammering shadows into friendly adventurers.
A clock that measures patience in grass-stains and loyal sighs,
windows that gleam with startled joy at every returning footstep.

A quiet hulk of weathered hunger for the sea’s wide mouth,
she wears tall, white-stretched ribs that catch the sigh of salt.
Her lungs fill with the breath of gulls and the drum of distant drums,
while timber nerves wake at the hinge of dawn and tide.
A clockwork chorus rattles the wakeful pine in her chest,
and inked horizons bow beneath her memory-scoured wake.

Moonlight leans on quiet paws, a velvet hush in dusk-tuned air.
Whiskers measure secrets, whisking thought between shadows soft as rain.
A hunter’s sigh threads through the chimney’s breath, then vanishes in a sigh again.
Eyes like chipped garnets drink the room, curling inward where warmth gathers.
Silk fur gathers dusk and returns it to the loom of night, unseen but near.

In the hush of pines, a quiet pulse treads the moss,
hooves sketching rain on needles, silvered with dawn.
Eyes like hidden pools invite the mist to linger,
antlers crown a whispering silhouette of wind and breath.

A dab of moon on slick, moist skin, they say, a sly reflection in dew-dark pools.
Hinges of ankles whisper spring in every quiet splash, a lantern blinking green.
Ribbed lungs stitched to chorus, a tremor of croon that threads night to water.
A compass of eyes learns the lone pulse of lilac dusk, then leaps where shadows widen.

A dawn rumor on sunlit legs, a heartbeat tripping the grass.
Lips tasting the air's breath, mane a brushing of wind-stirred rain.
 muscles coil like coiled springs under a dusk-tuned spine,
hooves drum a private rite on earth's smooth, patient skin.
A silhouette of thunder in a pasture's velvet breath,
it carries the distant whisper of roads unmade.

Silent thunder stitched from silver breath, it slices through a blue cathedral.
A gleaming needle threading runways of memory, it lifts without a rustle of wings.
Words rise in its wake—windows like watchful eyes, a heartbeat of turbines hum.
It carries strangers, unspooling mile markers, mapping the warmth between homes and rain.
Oiled sighs skim clouds, leaving contrails pale as forgotten handwriting in the sky.

A steel-gray heartbeat thrumps across the dawn-lit spine of roads,  
its breath a churn of exhaust and memory, clattering thoughts behind.  
A grave in reverse, carrying sun-worn maps and heavy promises,  
tires sigh on pavement like marching drums, cargo of mile-marked secrets.

Moonlit whiskers whisper along the edge of darkness,  
silken shadow-noise treading the quilt of night,  
a velvet hunt in breathless silence, ear to the slow hum of stars,  
pale paws sketching prayers on the edge of a warm window.

A quiet giant leans into the wind, listening to distant bells of foam.
A wooden breath, threaded with rope-thin prayers, cradles the waking tide.
A chorus of gulls sketches its memory on the edge of the world, always returning.
In its chest, a lantern heart keeps time with the moon’s patient tide.

A shadow slips through the lattice of the pines, keen-eyed in dusk’s hush.
Antlers crown a moonlit rumor, thumping soft in the undergrowth of breath.
Bounds like whispering frost, dusting the silver of a waking stream.
A heartbeat paddles through the thicket, delicate as a sigh at waking dawn.

A river of muscle under a sun-warmed hide,
hooves drum weather into the thirsty earth.
 mane a whisper of stormclouds brushing the dawn,
it gathers the horizon in a velvet mouth and runs. 
Breath spills like steam from a forge of steady grace,
a rider’s shadow learns to listen to the land.

A small tremor of dawn wears a velvet breath,  
feathers catching sun like shards of shy glass.  
It stitches the morning with quiet, clipped wings,  
and opens a throat that wears bells of rain.  
Skyward hunger tunes a hollow reed in flight,  
nibbling the wind until the horizon sighs.  
It leaves a chorus stitched to the map of air—  
a fleeting silhouette where thunder forgets its name.

A midnight pocket of rain and resilience sits in damp skin,
liquid chorus stitches the air, a tremor stitched to dampened moon.
A nimble surgeon of still ponds, leaping punctuation between reeds,
green heart thrums in a throat that keeps the world’s small, slippery secrets.
Eyes like two pale lanterns measure the world in ripples and hissed dew,
and every quiet breath becomes a puddle of patient patience.

A chorus of claws drums softly on the floor of rain,  
trembling at the door like a rumor of morning chased away.  
Warm breath bells the air with every hopeful reach,  
a damp-nosed compass that knows the street by scent and sound.  

Eyes bright as abandoned toys, loyal gravity in motion,  
tail a weather vane for storms of hello and goodbye.  
In sunlit chases, knotting grass into memory,  
the world leans closer, listening for a familiar heartbeat.

I harbor a cathedral of wind in silver skin,
carrying roomfuls of sighs above sleepy towns.
A mirrored sun rides its back, a restless heartbeat
that stitches miles with breath and rumors of thunder. 
It cups the atlas in quiet gravity, moonlit and bright,
then folds the map again, releasing dawn on distant doors.

A quiet silhouette steps soft as dusk, hunting the hush between lamps.

Paws untying quiet threads of shadow, fur a dusk-woven ember.
Eyes like moonlit coins weighing every sigh of the room.
Breath coils into warmth, a small drum beating against the dark.
Tiny throne of ambition perched on windowsills, surveying the world in furled patience.

A steel confined in a pocket of blue, measuring miles by breath.
It wears a whispering body that drinks the noon and sighs at dusk,

While it trades the world’s map for a single, keen hunger: distance.

A bronze heartbeat hums beneath a chassis dawn,

It wears a road as a second skin, whispering speed,

Below the gull-lit seam of dawn, a patient wanderer drinks the ocean’s breath,

A prow-memory carving its own dawn, chasing horizons like a rumor.

A silver dusk breathes through a living thunder,

A steel throat singing through a seam of clouds,

A quiet silhouette stitched of velvet dawn, I tread the twilight’s edge where footprints sigh.

Eyes, round weather vanes, track every shadowed rumor till dawn,

A sunrise, stitched in fur and warmth, that runs to greet the world and then returns.

A emerald sigh stitched to dinner-plate moons, it lingers by the edge of rain.

A metal heartbeat pulsing along a ribbon of tar,

A silver sigh cleaves the hollow of morning, bright as a rumor in the throat of light.

In a velvet meadow, a damp chorus keeps time,

A patient traveler with a timbered sighs of salt and wind,

 Lantern eyes blink along a darkened tide, counting the hours

A thunder of thunderclouds in a field of dust and root,

A silhouette of dusk with rain-silver eyes,

A lumbering rumor of steel and thunder,  
feet pressing the road into a whispered map.

Toward distant kitchens and warehouses it hums, patient, methodical, true.

A wandering loom of timber and sighs, it threads the salt-blue loom of night.

Hands on a compass of memory, it leans toward horizons like a patient friend.
The drumbeat of pine and rope keeps time with distant thunder’s ledger.

A waking hill of iron and bone, it heaves with patient sighs at dawn,

Carrying maps of distant weather within its chest of midnight glass.
